era_context: Early Bronze Age III; Old Kingdom Egypt (Dynasty 5–6 transition), Early Dynastic III Mesopotamia, mature Indus Valley Civilisation. No major civilisational transitions are expected within this year.

events.title: Old Kingdom Egypt: continued centralised rule and monument construction
events.description: During this period, Egypt's Old Kingdom maintained its characteristic cultural and administrative practices. Pyramid construction, mortuary temples, and administrative bureaucracy continued. The Pyramid Texts, inscribed in royal tombs, represent the oldest known religious literature in the world. Trade networks extended to the Levant, Nubia, and the Red Sea. No specific events are recorded for this year.
events.certainty_note: This entry represents era context, not a specific documented event.
